title: The ReAct pattern — Reason, Act, Observe, Repeat

soft_findings.description: Skill formula (words/230) yields 5.67 min, which is 0.33 min below the [6,8] floor. At the briefing-specified 200 wpm the value is 6.52 min (in range). Word count (1304) is within [1200,1600]. No hard fail triggered; the reading is substantively full-length.

format_discipline.notes: H1 is the first non-empty line. Sidecar reading.meta.json present and valid. Exactly 6 sections in correct order.

length.notes: Word count passes. Minutes depend on calculation basis: 230 wpm (skill canonical) gives 5.67 (below floor); 200 wpm (briefing basis) gives 6.52 (in range). Soft finding logged; not a hard fail.

citations.notes: All 5 primary resources cited at least once inline. Bibliography entries [1]–[5] present and match inline markers. No orphans.

no_forward_references.notes: No references to 14.7 (decision matrix) or 14.8 (agent safety) found in the reading.

sampled_assertions.assertion: ReAct = Reasoning + Acting loop structure interleaving Thought/Action/Observation
Four stages: Thought, Action, Observation, repeat until Final Answer
Thought is written as plain text with label 'Thought:' before every action
Observation comes from the tool, added by the system, not generated by the LLM
ReAct is primarily a prompting strategy; any capable instruction-following LLM can run it
LangChain implements ReAct as a first-class default agent loop
Hard cycle limit of 10–15 cycles is standard practice
Comparison table: generic 14.5 loop vs ReAct on reasoning visibility, debugging, failure modes
Eiffel Tower worked example: Cycle 1 search, Cycle 2 calculator, Cycle 3 Final Answer
Customer support: order DB + shipping API + policy retrieval tool chain with Thought audit trail

faithfulness.notes: All 10 sampled assertions trace directly to the corpus. No assertions introduced that are absent from the corpus.

beginner_readability.notes: LLM is expanded ('Large Language Model') on first use (line 49). LangChain is introduced with a plain-language description. All key terms defined at first appearance with bold+plain-meaning pattern. No hedging phrases found. Sentences are direct and short.

formatting.notes: No paragraph exceeds 6 sentences. The four ReAct stages are each rendered as named subsections with inline examples. Key Concepts uses bold-term / plain-meaning shape. Worked Example uses fenced code blocks and numbered list. Diagram embedded in Key Concepts section.

no_assessment_references.notes: No assessment IDs (A5), due weeks, or grade weights found in the reading.

scope.notes: Reading covers exactly: the four ReAct stages, comparison to the 14.5 loop, one worked trace, LangChain implementation, customer support pattern, and practical rules. No material beyond the topic headline. No forward-teaching of 14.7 or 14.8.

s7_2_multi_hop_gloss.finding: Corpus critic soft finding: check if 'multi-hop question answering' appears in the reading and is glossed.
s7_2_multi_hop_gloss.resolution: Term does not appear in the reading at all. The reading's In Practice section draws from corpus §7.1 (LangChain) and §7.3 (customer support), not §7.2. Omitting the multi-hop sub-section is appropriate scope discipline — the term and concept do not need a gloss because they are not used. Finding resolved.

gold_example_comparison.notes: Gold examples exist for topics 2-1 and 3-4. This reading matches the gold bar on: H1-first structure, 6-section template, bold-term definitions, inline examples preceding abstractions, diagram embedded in Key Concepts, and no assessment metadata. No deviation from gold-example format standards found.

escalation.reason: Zero scope_creep findings; checks 5-6 produce 0 distinct findings. Escalation threshold not reached.
